info.name: ABS ↔ Tally Voucher Sync
info.description:
Poll-and-acknowledge sync between ABS Cloud and Tally — masters (ledgers, items) and vouchers.

---

## Import order

Run masters **before** vouchers, so every `<LEDGER>` / `<STOCKITEM>` a voucher references already exists in Tally:

1. **Ledgers** — `GET /ledgers` → import → `POST /ledgers/acknowledge`
2. **Items** — `GET /items` → import → `POST /items/acknowledge`
3. **Vouchers** — `GET /vouchers` → import → `POST /acknowledge`

## The poll-and-acknowledge contract (same for all three)

1. **Pull** — GET returns rows not yet imported (`tally_synced_at IS NULL`), oldest first, paginated.
2. **Import** — Tally converts each JSON row into its native XML and imports it locally.
3. **Acknowledge** — POST back only the IDs that imported successfully. They are stamped `tally_synced_at` and drop out of future pulls.
4. Failed IDs are simply *not* acknowledged, so they re-appear on the next poll — nothing is ever lost. Acknowledge is idempotent.

Add `include_all=1` to any GET to also return already-acknowledged rows (re-import / audit).

## Auth

Every request needs the header `x-tally-key`. It is applied automatically at collection level from the `tally_key` variable.

Default key: `abs-tally-2024` — override by setting `TALLY_SYNC_KEY` in the backend `.env`.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `base_url` | `http://localhost:5000` | Backend origin |
| `tally_key` | `abs-tally-2024` | Value for the `x-tally-key` header |
| `voucher_ids` / `item_ids` / `ledger_ids` | *(auto)* | Filled by each Pull request's test script; consumed by its Acknowledge |

item.name: 1 · Masters — Ledgers
item.description: Ledger masters — one row per customer record (this app's ledger master; voucher `party_name` and every `ledger_entries` row reference it). Import these FIRST.

exec: console.log(`Pulled ${ids.length} ledger(s) of ${body.total} pending — ids saved to {{ledger_ids}}`);

request.description: Returns ledgers where `tally_synced_at IS NULL`. Each row carries everything a Tally `<LEDGER>` master needs: name, group, GSTIN, full address with resolved state, contact, opening balance (+ Dr/Cr) and the bill-by-bill flag. The test script saves the returned IDs into `{{ledger_ids}}`.

response.name: 200 — pending ledgers
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "total": 8894,
  "page": 1,
  "limit": 100,
  "ledgers": [
    {
      "id": 95,
      "ledger_name": "AJAY KR MAJHI",
      "ledger_group": "Sundry Debtors",
      "gstin": null,
      "address": {
        "address1": "Red cross road, graham bazar",
        "address2": null,
        "address3": "Dibrugarh",
        "city": "Dibrugarh",
        "state": "Assam",
        "pincode": "786001"
      },
      "contact_person": "Ajay",
      "mobile": "9101810600",
      "email": "kumar.ajay9004@gmail.com",
      "opening_balance": 0,
      "opening_balance_type": null,
      "bill_by_bill": true,
      "active": true,
      "created_at": null,
      "tally_synced_at": null
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Stamps `tally_synced_at` on each ledger ID. Body defaults to `{{ledger_ids}}` (auto-filled by *Pull unsynced ledgers*). Idempotent — already-acknowledged IDs are skipped.

item.name: 2 · Masters — Items
item.description: Stock item masters. Import these SECOND — before vouchers — so every `<STOCKITEM>` a voucher's inventory line references already exists.

exec: console.log(`Pulled ${ids.length} item(s) of ${body.total} pending — ids saved to {{item_ids}}`);

request.description: Returns items where `tally_synced_at IS NULL`. Each row carries name, group, category, Tally flavour, GST rate, HSN, `batch_tracked` (true = maintain batches/serials in Tally) and opening qty/rate/value. The test script saves the returned IDs into `{{item_ids}}`.

response.name: 200 — pending items
response.body:

request.description: Stamps `tally_synced_at` on each item ID. Body defaults to `{{item_ids}}` (auto-filled by *Pull unsynced items*). Idempotent — already-acknowledged IDs are skipped.

item.name: 3 · Vouchers — Pull
item.description: Fetch vouchers waiting to be imported into Tally. Run AFTER masters so referenced ledgers/items exist.

exec: // Stash the pulled IDs so '2 · Acknowledge' can send them back in one click.
console.log(`Pulled ${ids.length} voucher(s) of ${body.total} pending — ids saved to {{voucher_ids}}`);

query.description: Sales | Purchase | Payment | Receipt | Journal | Contra (matches parent type)

request.description:
Returns vouchers where `tally_synced_at IS NULL`, oldest first.

Enable the disabled query params to filter. The test script saves the returned IDs into `{{voucher_ids}}` for the Acknowledge request.

response.name: 200 — one pending Sales voucher

request.description: Unsynced vouchers restricted to an FY quarter (or any window). Dates filter on `vch_date`.

query.description: Sales | Purchase | Payment | Receipt | Journal | Contra

request.description: Filters on the *parent* voucher type — e.g. `vch_type=Sales` also matches custom sub-types like Tax Invoice.
Ignores the `tally_synced_at` filter — returns acknowledged vouchers too (each carries its `tally_synced_at` timestamp). Use for re-import after a Tally data loss, or to audit what was exported.

item.name: 4 · Vouchers — Acknowledge
item.description: Confirm which vouchers Tally imported successfully.

request.description:
Stamps `tally_synced_at = NOW()` on each ID so it drops out of future pulls.

The body defaults to `{{voucher_ids}}` — auto-filled by the *Pull unsynced vouchers* test script — so running Pull → Acknowledge back-to-back completes a full sync cycle. Replace with an explicit array (e.g. `[1, 2, 3]`) to acknowledge manually.

**Idempotent**: already-acknowledged IDs are skipped silently; `acknowledged` in the response counts only newly-stamped rows. Only acknowledge IDs that actually imported — anything you leave out is re-served on the next pull.

response.name: 400 — empty or invalid ids
